ks1.objective: Dribble in the middle. When your colour is called, take your ball to the matching corner.
successCriteria: Keeps the ball close while dribbling
Stops the ball with the sole of the foot at the station
Heads back to the middle quickly

lks2.objective: Develop quick directional dribbling and sole-of-foot control under coach calls.
successCriteria: Stops the ball cleanly with the sole

uks2.objective: Combine reactive dribbling with weaker-foot control and protect the ball from a Power Thief.
successCriteria: Uses weaker foot or completes a turn before stopping

space.description: Place two cones in each corner of the area to create four Power Stations. Enough space in the middle for children to dribble freely and safely.

value.name: Positivity
value.text: Recharging your positivity and energy. Everyone has moments where their energy feels low or things aren't going their way — that's normal. Just like in the game, sometimes we need to recharge before we go again. Simple ways to recharge: take a breath, have a drink, encourage a teammate, reset your focus.

steps: Children dribble freely inside the area with their football.
When a colour is called, they must dribble to the matching Power Station and stop their ball using the sole of their foot.
Once they've recharged, challenge them to explode back into the middle and continue dribbling.
Keep heads up, travel at speed, and stay in control of the football.
progressions: Visit the Power Stations in a specific order called out by the coach.
Complete a simple ball mastery task at the station before returning.
See how many Power Stations can be visited in 60 seconds.
Beat your own score from the previous round.
Introduce a Power Thief who tries to tag children travelling to a station — if tagged, complete a quick ball mastery task before rejoining.
questions: How do you know when you need to 'recharge'?
What does it look like when you stay in control at speed?
What's the quickest way to get out of a Power Station?

step.space: Smaller area or smaller stopping zones at each Power Station
step.task: Use weaker foot only, or complete a turn before stopping the ball
step.equipment: Reduce the number of Power Stations to create more traffic in the middle
step.people: Add defenders to guard Power Stations

base.showmanship: Use countdowns to create urgency and excitement. Commentate on fast dribbles, sharp turns and children travelling at speed. Use children's names regularly and praise effort loudly so parents can hear.
